head.hi: Chargé Hibben to the Secretary of State.

div.p:
Sir: Referring to my telegram of the 28th ultimoNot printed. relating to an intimation from the minister for foreign affairs in regard to the date for the opening of the International Opium Confernce, and to my
cablegram of to-day’s date,Not printed. I have the honor to inclose herewith a copy and translation of a note, just received, from Mr. Van Swinderin, in which he advises me that the Netherlands Government proposes Wednesday, the 30th of May, 1911, as the date for the meeting of the conference in question.

head.hi: The Minister for Foreign Affairs to Chargé Hibben.

opener.dateline:
Ministry for Foreign Affairs,
The Hague, November 1, 1910.

frus:attachment.p: Mr. Chargé d’affaires: Referring to your note of the 19th of October last, file 30020.18, I have the honor to inform you that Her Majesty’s Government proposes as date for the opening of the International Conference for Prevention of the Abuse of Opium, Wednesday, the 30th of May, 1911.
In adding that the States intending to participate in the conference have been notified of the above,
